title: Seed-your-profile phase (phase 3) — path choice, bootstrap prompt, and profile-bootstrap handoff

impl.notes: PhaseSeeding replaces the placeholder 'About you' phase 3. BootstrapCopyBlock is an inline copy component (raw-content, not buildHandoff) because the bootstrap prompt is a full markdown document, not a structured handoff. The 'profile-bootstrap' kind string appears in the BootstrapCopyBlock label and in handoff.ts HandoffKind union. Polling is 5-second setInterval cleared on component unmount or when profileDetected becomes true. verificationPlan[2] was corrected from the stale reconstruction-marker check to the AC6-compliant FULL_NAME count check — QA should note that `grep -i 'OQ-005' templates/bootstrap-profile-prompt.md` WILL find a line (the file header cites OQ-005 as a resolved reference), but this is NOT a pending reconstruction marker; AC6 is satisfied.

verificationRun.result: PASS — client and server build clean, check-profile-exists and read-bootstrap-prompt appear in server bundle

userStory: As a user setting up my Personal OS, I want to choose between bootstrapping my profile via Glean and completing it manually, so that I get rich, research-grounded context when Glean is available and a clear manual fallback when it is not.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Phase 3 presents two clearly labelled options: 'Bootstrap with Glean' and 'Set up manually'. The user's selection is persisted in onboarding-state.json under key `seedingPath` with value `'bootstrap'` or `'manual'`. `cat docs/data/local/onboarding-state.json | grep seedingPath` returns one of these values after the choice is made.

The 'Bootstrap with Glean' option is visually disabled and displays the note "Complete assistant wiring in phase 2 first" when `completedPhases` in onboarding-state.json does not include phase 2 (per story 005 AC6). The option becomes active only after phase 2 is marked complete.

Selecting 'Bootstrap with Glean' displays the master-prompt block from `templates/bootstrap-profile-prompt.md` (Personal OS wrapper + master prompt) with the user's full name and work email substituted for the [FULL_NAME] and [WORK_EMAIL] placeholders — the phase asks for both before the prompt is shown. The prompt block is delivered via Collect & Copy with handoff kind 'profile-bootstrap'.

After the bootstrap Collect & Copy block is shown, a waiting/refresh area appears with instruction text stating that the user should return after their assistant has written context/profile.md, and either a 'Refresh' button or an auto-detect poll (every 5 seconds) transitions the phase to the waiting-resolved state when context/profile.md appears on disk.
Selecting 'Set up manually' records `seedingPath: 'manual'` in onboarding-state.json and enables the 'Next phase' button, advancing to phase 4 with the manual interview forms (story 003).

`templates/bootstrap-profile-prompt.md` is committed and carries the production prompt text supplied via OQ-005 (resolved): `grep -c 'FULL_NAME' templates/bootstrap-profile-prompt.md` returns at least 2, and no reconstruction/placeholder-pending marker appears in the file.

technicalNotes: Add phase 3 component (PhaseSeeding) to `work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx`. Read `templates/bootstrap-profile-prompt.md` server-side to render the prompt with name substitution; expose via a server function or static import. `seedingPath` is stored in `answers` in onboarding-state.json (story 001). Profile auto-detect polls for `context/profile.md` existence via a lightweight server function (e.g. `check-profile-exists`). The template file `templates/bootstrap-profile-prompt.md` is authored by the implementer — this story describes the required structure; the implementer creates the file. Do NOT use real profile data from the worked example; any structural mock data used in development must use a fictional persona.
outOfScope: The Review & correct section cards (story 010), profile corrections handoff (story 011), the manual interview forms content (story 003), creating the actual prompt text (pending OQ-005).

verificationPlan.checks: Returns a line containing 'bootstrap' or 'manual' confirming the path choice is persisted.
Returns a match confirming the bootstrap handoff kind is wired in the phase 3 component.

Returns at least 2, confirming the production prompt with FULL_NAME placeholders is present (AC6). Note: the original entry here checked for a reconstruction marker — that was stale; AC6 was revised when OQ-005 resolved and the production prompt was committed.

revisionLog.change: AC3 now requires both [FULL_NAME] and [WORK_EMAIL] substitution; AC6 flipped from reconstruction-marker to production-text requirement.
revisionLog.reason: OQ-005 resolved — the production bootstrap prompt was supplied and captured in templates/bootstrap-profile-prompt.md.
